title: Caleana triens
taxonomy: Caleana triens is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and is native to Australia. The genus Caleana is a small group of orchids that are endemic to Western Australia. Caleana triens is a terrestrial orchid that grows in a range of habitats, from woodlands to heathlands. It is often found growing in association with other plant species, such as grasses and shrubs.
uses: Caleana triens, also known as the Three-Winged Spider Orchid, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. intro:

Calea (Calea spp.) is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as fleabane or Mexican damiana. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Calea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Calea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia.
